commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a gateway maintenance scheduler for daily session resets, wires its timer through startup/shutdown, adds focused tests, and updates session docs.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main lacks a maintenance sweep, and the PR finding is source-reproducible because the scheduler scans one store but the reset service re-resolves the backing store from the key.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can bind scheduled resets to the scanned store and add duplicate-store coverage; maintainer acceptance of proactive rollover remains a merge decision afterward.

target.title: fix(gateway): run daily session resets on schedule
target.body:
## Summary

- Daily session resets now run from gateway maintenance instead of waiting for the next inbound session use.
- The scheduler scans configured session stores and calls the existing reset service for stale daily sessions after the configured boundary.
- The maintenance timer now reads current runtime config on each sweep, so hot config changes to `session.reset`, `session.resetByType`, `session.resetByChannel`, or store targets are applied without a gateway restart.
- Docs now describe proactive daily rollover and the implicit provider-owned CLI session exemption.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra
- [x] Docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #82321
- Related: N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

A daily reset with `atHour` is a wall-clock policy. Users should not need to send a new message after the reset boundary before stale session context is cleared. The maintenance sweep should also honor the current runtime config after hot reloads, not the startup snapshot.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Daily sessions stale past `atHour` are reset by gateway maintenance without waiting for a new inbound message, while active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are preserved.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout in a Linux container, on branch `fix-daily-session-reset`, running the patched gateway maintenance timer against an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`. Bundled plugin facades were disabled for the proof process with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` so the reset service did not depend on source-checkout plugin artifacts.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Ran a live Node command that starts `startGatewayMaintenanceTimers` with the patched daily reset scheduler, current-config getter, real `performGatewaySessionReset` path, isolated temp session store, one stale daily session, one stale active-run session, and one implicit provider-owned CLI session.
  - Command shape: `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... startGatewayMaintenanceTimers(...) ... TS`
  - Supplemental verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts`
  - Supplemental type gates: `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp> OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # creates isolated agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  # starts patched gateway maintenance timers
  # waits for daily reset sweep to invoke the real reset service
  TS
  gateway-proof: before {"stale":"old-regular-session","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  [session-daily-reset] daily session reset sweep complete
  gateway-proof: after {"stale":"5f959c12-f0ed-4ed1-99d2-cc158c66ac87","active":"old-active-session","cli":"old-cli-session"}
  gateway-proof: staleReset true
  gateway-proof: activePreserved true
  gateway-proof: implicitCliPreserved true

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests       18 passed (18)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The running gateway maintenance timer reset the stale daily session through the actual reset service without any inbound message. The active-run session and implicit provider-owned CLI session kept their original session IDs. Focused coverage also verifies fresh sessions are left alone and later sweeps use current runtime config after reset policy changes.
- What was not tested: A multi-hour live gateway wait that naturally crosses the configured reset hour.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Daily reset freshness was only evaluated through session-use paths. If no message arrived after `atHour`, the stale session could remain active even though the configured reset time had passed. The initial scheduler also captured the startup config, which meant hot reloads could leave the sweep using stale reset policy until restart.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/session-daily-reset-scheduler.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-maintenance.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - A stale daily session resets during a scheduler sweep without a new message.
  - A fresh daily session is left alone.
  - Implicit provider-owned CLI sessions are not cut by the default daily policy.
  - Sessions with active runs are skipped.
  - Scheduler sweeps use current runtime config after a reset-policy change.
- Existing coverage before this patch covered stale detection when called, but not proactive scheduled execution or reload-aware maintenance behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Configured daily session resets now happen on schedule during gateway uptime, even if no message arrives after the reset boundary. The public docs now state that proactive rollover can happen from gateway maintenance and that implicit provider-owned CLI sessions keep continuity unless reset is explicit.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
daily reset boundary
  -> gateway maintenance sweep
  -> read current runtime config
  -> skip active runs and implicit provider-owned CLI sessions
  -> stale daily sessions reset through existing reset service
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container
- Runtime/container: Local OpenClaw checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `session.reset.mode=daily`, `session.reset.atHour=4`

### Steps

1. Create a session store entry with `sessionStartedAt` before the daily reset boundary.
2. Run the daily reset scheduler scan with `nowMs` after the reset boundary.
3. Confirm the reset callback runs for the stale session.
4. Repeat with a fresh post-boundary session and confirm it is not reset.
5. Mark a stale session key active and confirm it is skipped.
6. Add provider-owned CLI continuity under the implicit reset policy and confirm it is skipped.
7. Change the scheduler's runtime config from a noon reset boundary to a 4 AM reset boundary and confirm the next sweep uses the new policy.

### Expected

Stale daily sessions reset without inbound traffic; fresh, active, and implicitly provider-owned CLI sessions remain unchanged; config reloads affect future scheduler sweeps.

### Actual

The focused scheduler and gateway maintenance tests match that behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Focused scheduler and maintenance test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.
- Docs updated in:
  - `docs/concepts/session.md`
  - `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`
  - `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md`

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: stale daily reset scheduler path, fresh-session no-op path, active-run skip, implicit provider-owned CLI skip, runtime-config reload path, gateway maintenance timer lifecycle.
- Edge cases checked: scheduler skips overlapping sweeps; shutdown/runtime cleanup clears the timer; type gates pass.
- Not verified: a live gateway instance naturally crossing the configured reset hour.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the gateway now scans configured session stores every minute.
- Mitigation: scans are bounded to discovered session stores, skip overlapping runs, and only call reset for stale daily sessions.
- Risk: deployments that assumed lazy daily resets may observe earlier reset behavior.
- Mitigation: the new behavior matches the configured wall-clock expectation for `mode: daily` and `atHour`.
- Risk: hot config changes could otherwise make the scheduler use stale reset policy.
- Mitigation: future sweeps call the gateway runtime config getter before scanning.
target.author: stevenepalmer

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: latensified

target.title: telegram: support expandable final blockquotes
target.body:
## Summary - Adds Telegram formatter support for Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Preserves existing normal blockquote rendering. - Adds formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. ## Changes - Render expandable blockquote metadata as `
`. - Preserve existing blockquote rendering behavior. - Add formatter coverage for expandable blockquotes. - Problem: OpenClaw's Telegram formatter could not represent Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form. - Solution: Add explicit formatter support for expandable blockquotes. - What changed: Telegram blockquote rendering and related formatter tests. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Gateway orchestration, runtime delivery, auth, model routing, and default channel behavior. ## Motivation Telegram supports expandable blockquotes natively. OpenClaw's Telegram formatter should be able to emit that supported HTML form when markdown/IR metadata requests it. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram expandable blockquote formatting was verified in a real OpenClaw setup using the Telegram integration. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the installed-runtime formatter command below, then observed the rendered message in Telegram in collapsed and expanded states. - Evidence after fix: Live formatter command output plus Telegram screenshots: - Collapsed screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b590a21f-5b43-4bea-b917-9bba48fa42e2 - Expanded screenshot: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77508c27-954a-40bb-a0ae-e4666a08d95e - Console output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` - Observed result after fix: Telegram displayed the trailing blockquote collapsed initially and expanded it in-place when tapped. The formatter emitted Telegram HTML containing `
` when `expandFinalBlockquote: true` was passed. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. Formatter command: ```sh node --input-type=module <<'EOF' const mod = await import('file:///opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/format-AZK6G0is.js'); const sample = [ 'DM expandable signature test', '', '> Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01', '> GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification', '> Grok: none', '> Ollama-14B: none', '> session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535' ].join('\n'); console.log(mod.a(sample, { textMode: 'markdown', expandFinalBlockquote: true })); EOF ``` Formatter output: ```html
Usage: 1 in / 1 out · cache 0/0 · est $0.01 GPT-5.5: expandable signature verification Grok: none Ollama-14B: none session agent:main:telegram:direct:7456384535
``` ## Verification - Ran the Telegram formatter tests. - Verified live Telegram rendering in a real OpenClaw installation. - Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram formatter can now emit native expandable blockquote HTML. - Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.18 installed runtime on macOS with Telegram channel enabled. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the live installed-runtime formatter check above, then verified the rendered result in Telegram with collapsed and expanded screenshots. - Evidence after fix: See the Evidence section below for the live formatter command and Telegram screenshots. - Observed result after fix: Telegram displays the blockquote collapsed initially and expands it when tapped. - What was not tested: Other Telegram clients beyond the tested client. - Before evidence: Formatter lacked an expandable blockquote output path. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: The Telegram formatter only emitted normal blockquote HTML and did not carry expandable blockquote metadata through to Telegram's native `
` form. - Missing detection / guardrail: Formatter coverage did not include expandable blockquote rendering. - Contributing context (if known): N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [ ] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: Telegram formatter tests. - Scenario the test should lock in: Expandable blockquote metadata renders as `
`, while normal blockquotes continue rendering normally. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The behavior is a deterministic formatter output decision. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing blockquote formatter coverage covered normal blockquotes. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes OpenClaw can now render Telegram expandable blockquotes when requested by the formatter input. ## Diagram (if applicable) N/A ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local OpenClaw install - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram - Relevant config (redacted): Telegram channel enabled ### Steps 1. Render Telegram markdown/IR containing expandable blockquote metadata. 2. Send or observe the rendered output in Telegram. 3. Expand the blockquote in Telegram. ### Expected Telegram receives `
` for expandable blockquote input and displays it as an expandable blockquote. ### Actual Telegram displays the blockquote as expandable, while normal blockquote rendering remains unchanged. ## Evidence - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Collapsed: Expanded: ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: Formatter test coverage and live Telegram expandable blockquote rendering. - Edge cases checked: Normal blockquote rendering remains normal. - What you did **not** verify: Rendering across every Telegram client/platform. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`No`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Telegram HTML support may vary slightly across Telegram clients. - Mitigation: The change uses Telegram's native expandable blockquote HTML form and keeps existing normal blockquote behavior unchanged.
target.author: latensified
